L1.area: Resource loading and extraction within the HTML presentation layer

L2.description: A race condition vulnerability exists in the temporary directory creation logic. A temporary file is first created, then deleted, and a directory is created at the same path. The time gap between the file deletion and directory creation allows a local attacker to create a symbolic link at the path, potentially leading to local privilege escalation.

L3.area: Resource extraction in the WebKit presenter subproject
L3.description: A race condition vulnerability exists in the temporary directory creation logic used during JAR resource extraction in the WebKit presenter. A temporary file is first created, then deleted, and a directory is created at the same path. The time gap between the file deletion and directory creation allows a local attacker to create a symbolic link at that path, potentially leading to local privilege escalation when extracting JAR archive contents.

ground_truth.reason: A TOCTOU race condition exists where a temporary file is created and deleted before creating a directory at the same path, allowing a local attacker to insert a symbolic link during the time gap, leading to potential local privilege escalation.
